Computer-Assisted Reporting Newsletter, 1990–2007

Browse Topics Search

Like Money in the Bank

John Hill Sarasota Herald-Tribune August 1996
Demographics & Census

A 1995 Herald-Tribune investigation using HMDA data revealed that while banks claimed improvements in racial lending disparities, Black applicants actually experienced only marginal gains and higher application dropout rates, with credit becoming more concentrated along racial lines.

In July 1995, the Sarasota Herald-Tribune prepared a story showing racial disparities in home lending using HMDA data from 1993. After being told improvements happened in 1994, analysis showed only marginal gains for blacks and a high dropout rate in their applications. The new schemes actually concentrated housing credit more along racial lines.
